domain: cetacean communication (sperm whale codas, DSWP/Sharma 2024, eris-ketos) -- a2_probe flip characterization + DRI=Bond-Index reconciliation
data: DominicaCodas.csv (sw-combinatoriality): 8718 codas; ICI rhythm features (nClicks, Duration, ICI1..9); labels CodaType/Clan/Unit
consumer: Clan (dialect) classification from coda rhythm -- held-out acc 0.977 (majority 0.893; 2 clans EC1/EC2, imbalanced); 11-dim features

a2_diagnostics.regime: isotropic BUT cross-channel-correlated (ICIs temporally correlated) -> WHITENING-FRIENDLY

finding: The SPD-whitened family (the a2_probe candidate added in turboquant-pro PR #159) WINS AT EVERY bit level -- the clearest domain for it -- because the coda ICI rhythm features are cross-channel CORRELATED, which the diagonal per-channel code cannot decorrelate but ZCA whitening does. New regime point: isotropic (unit_disp 1.41) yet correlated -> whitening-friendly (distinct from KV-keys concentrated-correlated and legal channel-structured).
dri_reconciliation: eris-ketos DecoderRobustnessIndex is EXPLICITLY 'Bond Index (ethics) -> DRI (bioacoustics)' -- the same consumer-relative distortion methodology: invariant acoustic transforms must not change the decoder read (ker P_C); stress transforms do (the read direction). eris-ketos already instantiates observation theory for whale decoders; the DRI acts on audio, the a2_probe flip on the extracted coda features.
caveats: 11-dim features (low); Clan consumer imbalanced (89% EC1); audio-level DRI needs raw audio (only coda features available); CodaType is near-tautological from ICIs (unusable consumer).
